source_refs.snippet:
commit 584f396132aa19d21bb1e38ad9a5d428869290cb
Author:     Reimar Döffinger <Reimar.Doeffinger@gmx.de>
AuthorDate: Mon Jul 6 09:32:17 2020 +0200
Commit:     Guo, Yejun <yejun.guo@intel.com>
CommitDate: Mon Jul 6 20:22:30 2020 +0800

    dnn_backend_native: Add overflow check for length calculation.
    
    We should not silently allocate an incorrect sized buffer.
    Fixes trac issue #8718.
    
    Signed-off-by: Reimar Döffinger <Reimar.Doeffinger@gmx.de>
    Reviewed-by: Michael Niedermaye

commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author:     Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
AuthorDate: Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800
Commit:     Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
CommitDate: Fri Feb 18 16:54:11 2022 +1100

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of vid

commit d254fe2d1da3b1cba3526c5d6417c9912e330988
Author:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
AuthorDate: Thu Oct 19 22:07:36 2023 +0200
Commit:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
CommitDate: Sun Apr 14 22:12:33 2024 +0200

    avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
    
    Fixes: out of array access
    Fixes: rtpdec_h264.c149/poc
    
    Found-by: Hardik Shah of Vehere
    Reviewed-by: Martin Storsjö <martin@martin.st>
    Signed-off-by: Michael Niedermayer <michae

commit e4d5ac8d7d2a08658b3db7dd821246fe6b35381f
Author:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
AuthorDate: Thu Oct 19 22:07:36 2023 +0200
Commit:     Michael Niedermayer <michael@niedermayer.cc>
CommitDate: Fri Oct 27 18:10:47 2023 +0200

    avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
    
    Fixes: out of array access
    Fixes: rtpdec_h264.c149/poc
    
    Found-by: Hardik Shah of Vehere
    Reviewed-by: Martin Storsjö <martin@martin.st>
    Signed-off-by: Michael Niedermayer <michae

content.stdout_excerpt:
commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

656cb0450a avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
f025b8e110 avcodec/threadframe: Add ff_thread_(get|release)_ext_buffer()
b3551b6072 avcodec/thread: Move ff_thread_(await|report)_progress to new header
46309f262c avcodec/vp3: Don't output bogus warning
96caa01f13 avcodec/vp3: Check version in all cases when VP4 code is not built

content.text:
commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

656cb0450a avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
f025b8e110 avcodec/threadframe: Add ff_thread_(get|release)_ext_buffer()
b3551b6072 avcodec/thread: Move ff_thread_(await|report)_progress to new header
46309f262c avcodec/vp3: Don't output bogus warning
96caa01f13 avcodec/vp3: Check version in all cases when VP4 code is not built

content.snippet:
commit 656cb0450aeb73b25d7d26980af342b37ac4c568
Author: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>
Date:   Tue Feb 15 17:58:08 2022 +0800

    avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
    
    Since the av_malloc() may fail and return NULL pointer,
    it is needed that the 's->edge_emu_buffer' should be checked
    whether the new allocation is success.
    
    Fixes: d14723861b ("VP3: fix decoding of videos with stride > 2048")
    Reviewed-by: Peter Ross <pross@xvid.org>
    Signed-off-by: Jiasheng Jiang <jiasheng@iscas.ac.cn>

 libavcodec/vp3.c | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

content.claim: git log --follow around changed path libavcodec/vp3.c
content.snippet:
656cb0450a avcodec/vp3: Add missing check for av_malloc
f025b8e110 avcodec/threadframe: Add ff_thread_(get|release)_ext_buffer()
b3551b6072 avcodec/thread: Move ff_thread_(await|report)_progress to new header
46309f262c avcodec/vp3: Don't output bogus warning
96caa01f13 avcodec/vp3: Check version in all cases when VP4 code is not built

content.description: av_malloc return value is not checked for NULL; if allocation fails, s->edge_emu_buffer remains NULL and will be dereferenced
content.statement: The return value of av_malloc() is not checked. If av_malloc fails (returns NULL), s->edge_emu_buffer is NULL, causing a NULL pointer dereference on subsequent use.

content.description: Added NULL check after av_malloc, returning AVERROR(ENOMEM) and jumping to error label on failure
content.statement: The fix adds a NULL check for s->edge_emu_buffer after the av_malloc call, returning AVERROR(ENOMEM) and jumping to the error label if allocation fails, preventing NULL pointer dereference.

content.summary: Missing NULL check after av_malloc in vp3_decode_frame leads to NULL pointer dereference
content.mechanism: In libavcodec/vp3.c, function vp3_decode_frame, after allocating edge_emu_buffer via av_malloc, the code did not check whether the allocation returned NULL. If av_malloc fails (returns NULL), subsequent usage of s->edge_emu_buffer will cause a NULL pointer dereference (CWE-476). The fix adds a NULL check after av_malloc, returning AVERROR(ENOMEM) on failure.

content.snippet:
d254fe2d1d avformat/rtsp: Use rtsp_st->stream_index
ea9418debc avformat/rtsp: break on unknown protocols
d1e8be3411 Revert "avformat/rtp: Pass sources and block filter addresses via sdp file for rtp"
635ca9aa01 avformat/rtp: Pass sources and block filter addresses via sdp file for rtp
289838b7bd libavformat/rtsp: return error if rtsp_hd_out is null instead of crash

content.description: parse_sdp_a_line was called with loop index `i` as the stream argument instead of rtsp_st->stream_index, causing the callee to read an out-of-bounds stream entry from s->streams[]
content.statement: rtsp_st->dynamic_handler->parse_sdp_a_line(s, i, rtsp_st->dynamic_protocol_context, line) — the second argument is the RTSP-stream loop index `i`, which is not guaranteed to match the AVStream index in s->streams[]

content.description: The fix replaces the loop counter `i` with rtsp_st->stream_index so that parse_sdp_a_line receives the correct AVStream index, preventing out-of-bounds access
content.statement: rtsp_st->dynamic_handler->parse_sdp_a_line(s, rtsp_st->stream_index, rtsp_st->dynamic_protocol_context, line) — uses the correct stream_index field instead of the loop variable

content.summary: Out-of-bounds array access in parse_fmtp due to using loop index instead of stream_index
content.mechanism: In parse_fmtp, the second argument passed to rtsp_st->dynamic_handler->parse_sdp_a_line was the RTSP-stream loop counter `i` (an index into rt->rtsp_streams[]) instead of rtsp_st->stream_index (the actual AVStream index in s->streams[]). When parse_sdp_a_line uses this value as an index into the format context's stream array, the mismatched index can exceed the bounds of s->streams[], causing an out-of-bounds array access.

content.description: Only one function (parse_fmtp) was changed; other callers of parse_sdp_a_line may have similar bugs but are out of scope for this CVE.

content.reason: The exact callsite in parse_sdp_a_line that performs the out-of-bounds access is not visible from the patch hunk alone; the root cause is inferred from the parameter change.

content.description: calculate_operand_data_length multiplied four int32_t dims and sizeof(float) in int32_t arithmetic without overflow check, returning a silently wrapped result
content.statement: calculate_operand_data_length performed `oprd->dims[0] * oprd->dims[1] * oprd->dims[2] * oprd->dims[3] * sizeof(float)` using int32_t multiplication without overflow detection, allowing integer overflow to produce a truncated buffer length

content.description: Use uint64_t accumulator with per-iteration overflow guard against INT32_MAX, returning 0 on overflow
content.statement: calculate_operand_data_length now uses uint64_t `len`, multiplies iteratively, checks `len > INT32_MAX` each step, and returns 0 (signalling error to callers) if overflow is detected

content.summary: Integer overflow in calculate_operand_data_length leads to undersized buffer allocation
content.mechanism: calculate_operand_data_length performed oprd->dims[0] * oprd->dims[1] * oprd->dims[2] * oprd->dims[3] * sizeof(float) in int32_t arithmetic without overflow protection. Large dimension values cause the multiplication to wrap to a small positive or negative value. The truncated result is returned as int32_t and passed directly to av_malloc/av_realloc, allocating an undersized buffer that subsequent DNN layer execution writes past, resulting in a heap buffer overflow.

content.reason: The packet does not include a concrete reproducer model/input that triggers the overflow; trac#8718 is referenced but its contents are not provided
